resumeLength: One page for internship/new-grad recruiting; concise two-page resumes only for senior roles.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Readable serif or sans-serif resume font; keep one family and two weights at most.
formattingRules.margin: 0.5-0.75 inch for one-page resumes; 0.75-1 inch for academic CVs.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Single-level bullets, 1-2 lines each, parallel punctuation.
formattingRules.dateFormat: Consistent month/year or year-only format, right-aligned when layout supports it.

writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + finance analysis/model + scope + decision/risk/client outcome
quantificationRules: Map evidence to company signals: financial analysis, valuation, risk, markets, client service, execution.
Attach a number, scale, frequency, audience, runtime, revenue, cost, accuracy, conversion, latency, or time-to-completion metric whenever the source material supports it.
When exact metrics are absent, use bounded scope statements such as team size, dataset size, user group, deliverable count, or project duration.
Never invent metrics. Mark missing metrics as an improvement question instead of fabricating proof.
Prefer outcome metrics over task volume: revenue, adoption, accuracy, savings, cycle time, risk reduction, or decision quality.

exampleBullets.strong: Built DCF and trading-comps models for 8 software peers, stress-testing revenue assumptions across base, upside, and downside cases.
exampleBullets.reason: Names finance tools, peer scope, and analytical rigor.

exampleBullets.strong: Automated weekly portfolio risk report, cutting manual update time by 45% and improving exposure visibility for senior analysts.
exampleBullets.reason: Connects operational improvement to stakeholder value.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Most prominent bullets and sections map to the target role, company, and industry requirements.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and measurable outcome without unsupported claims.
Concise, specific, active language with low repetition and no inflated AI-like phrasing.
Structure, length, and emphasis match the selected guide/style profile.

promptSnippets.title: Goldman Sachs structure guardrail
promptSnippets.content: When applying Goldman Sachs rules, preserve conventional section names, prioritize the most relevant evidence first, and do not add unsupported content.

promptSnippets.title: Goldman Sachs bullet rewrite rule
promptSnippets.content: Rewrite bullets using Action + finance analysis/model + scope + decision/risk/client outcome; each bullet should start with a strong verb, name the method or tool, include scope, and end with an outcome when evidence exists.
